rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is continued to December 3, 2021. By no later than November 18, 2021 parties to provide courtesy copies of their respective supplemental briefs with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. Linda Z. Yu shall file a declaration no later than November 18, 2021 addressing failure to comply with this Court's October 4, 2021 Order directing the filing of the supplemental brief no later than October 22, 2021. Courtesy copy of the declaration to be delivered no later than November 19, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Declaring Whether Undertaking Is Required To Stay Distrib Of Funds Pending Appeal, Etc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER DECLARING WHETHER UNDERTAKING IS REQUIRED TO STAY DISTRIBUTION OF FUNDS PENDING APPEAL, ETC. is DENIED, moot as to the funds already distributed. The only remaining amounts are amounts ordered to be kept by the receiver and amounts deposited with the Court. The undertaking is required to stay the distribution of funds. CCP 917.1 and 917.2. Defendant shall have until November 14, 2021 to post an appropriate bond covering $1,610,000. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Allowance And Payment Of Its Attorneys Fees And Costs From The Receivership Estate As A Prevailing Party And As The Holder Of A Claim Against The Receivership Estate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ALLOWANCE AND PAYMENT OF ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS FROM THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE AS A PREVAILING PARTY AND AS THE HOLDER OF A CLAIM AGAINST THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE is GRANTED. Moving party established its entitlement to recovery of attorney's fees in this action and supported its request for payment of attorney's fees incurred in other cases out of the receivership estate. The amount of fees (including hourly rate and hours spent re: loadstar determination) is supported by evidence and legal authority. Late-filed, oversized opposition without tables of contents and authorities is stricken. Opposition was due on October 18, 2021 and was submitted for filing at 11:03 p.m. on October 20, 2021. Late-filed and oversized opposition did not support its arguments relevant to this motion with any authority on point (e.g. authority standing for the proposition that request for payment of fees out of receivership estate incurred in other cases must be made in a separate motion; authority that the Court should not enter an order authorizing recovery of fees because an appeal from such order may be taken; etc). Opposition did not specify a single line-item in the billing statements reflecting overbilling for a particular task performed (unreasonable rate or unreasonable hours for a task). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRENNAN 969 MARKET STREET LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The Court rules as follows on Defendant's objections to Plaintiff's evidence: (1) Objections to Best Deposition is overruled; (2) Objection 5 to Mark Brannan Declaration - sustained on relevance grounds under EC 350; (3) Objection 6 to John Brennan Declaration sustained on foundation grounds; (4) Objection 7 to Mark Brannan Declaration: (the court assumes that is a typographical error in the objection and the date is January 25, 2021, not January 15, 2021) - overruled as to the first sentence; and sustained as to the second sentence on relevancy grounds; objection to the evidence regarding January 31, 2019 - overruled as to the first sentence and sustained as to the second sentence. Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence under City of Oroville v. Superior Court of Butte County (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1091 re: City's liability for inverse condemnation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Ofmotion & Motion For Order Striking Untimely Amended Complaint And Dismissing Action For Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Sustained With Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT MARK SMITH'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING UNTIMELY AMENDED COMPLAINT AND DISMISSING ACTION FOR FAILURE TO AMEND COMPLAINT AFTER DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND is DENIED. Amended Complaint filed within 5 days of entry of order. Defendant was the prevailing party on the demurrer that was heard and sustained on August 27, 2021. The Court did not receive any proposed orders from the defendant, the prevailing party; the Court received a proposed order on September 22, 2021 prepared by Plaintiff per CRC 3.1312. An order sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend was entered on September 28, 2021. Amended Complaint was filed 2 days later on September 30, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JOHN KOURY VS. MATTHEW GRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 7. PLAINTIFF JOHN KOURY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact, including but not limited to intentional/mistaken water diversion, permission for alterations, etc. The Court notes that written opposition was not filed timely, the day before the original hearing date. Hearing continuance did not authorize late-filing of opposition. The Court will consider the written opposition in lieu of oral argument from Defendant. Plaintiff may present oral reply. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: VOZIKES 2017 LLC VS. BRANDON CAMPBELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANT BRANDON CAMPBELL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Based on the notice of motion the sole basis for relief is CCP 473(d). Moving party failed to show that the judgment is void within the meaning of this code section. Defendant may file a motion to vacate per CCP 473(b), CCP 473.5, etc. if such motion may be made in good faith. If defendant choses to do so, the motion may be filed on shortened time: moving papers to be filed and served no later than November 5, 2021 by noon; opposition to be filed and served no later than November 10, 2021; hearing on November 12, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
